input.headline: Major League Baseball signs deals with Netflix, ESPN and NBCUniversal

raw.headline: Major League Baseball signs deals with Netflix, ESPN and NBCUniversal

winner: Why did Netflix, ESPN, and NBCUniversal sign contracts with Major League Baseball? Because they wanted to stream every pitch live, strike, ball, and error in the world!

candidates.text: Why did Major League Baseball sign deals with Netflix, ESPN, and NBCUniversal? To ensure that even if a game is dull, it can still be binge-watched!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "The joke is concise, surprising, and specific to the headline, with a clever twist on binge-watching despite potential dullness of games."}

candidates.text: Why did MLB sign deals with Netflix, ESPN, and NBCUniversal? Because now they want viewers to "strike out" at home!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 7.5, "reason": "The joke is clever with a play on 'strike out' from baseball, but the punchline could be more tightly connected to the deals mentioned."}

candidates.text: Why was Netflix, ESPN, and NBCUniversal all at the ballpark? Because they heard Major League Baseball was signing streaming deals.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat clever with the twist of streaming services at a ballpark, but it's a bit generic and relies heavily on the setup rather than a punchline."}

candidates.text: Why did Major League Baseball sign contracts with Netflix, ESPN, and NBCUniversal? Because they wanted to ensure that every home run would now come with a streaming bonus!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat creative but lacks strong surprise or specificity. The 'streaming bonus' idea is a mild pun but doesn't fully tie into the logic of why MLB would sign with those specific companies."}

candidates.text: Why did Netflix, ESPN, and NBCUniversal sign contracts with Major League Baseball? Because they wanted to stream every pitch live, strike, ball, and error in the world!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat specific and uses wordplay on 'stream' and baseball terms, but it's a bit generic and lacks the sharp surprise or originality that would make it stand out in a humor competition."}
